How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-07-02 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Flags tickers appearing in 2 or more coherent screeners within one trading day at score 65-plus, with validated combos ranked first.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

HUM leads the batch at a score of 1475, hitting CLENOW_MOMENTUM, PULLBACK, and VOLUME_LEADER simultaneously, followed by PANW at 865 on the exact same triple. ALAB at 189 is the only name with four screeners, including TRAP_SHORT, which implies active short pressure running into a breakout setup. The score distribution is aggressively top-heavy: HUM at 1475 against a ninth-place score of 100 means most of the 20 overlaps are marginal filler. No ticker today matched a VALID validated combo, so none cleared the SMART_FLOW x UNUSUAL_V3 or 3-plus VALID screener hurdles that carry real statistical weight, and the NEUTRAL macro regime at 50.86 adds no directional tailwind. Take HUM first on score dominance, at reduced conviction given the missing validated combo.

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: CAT (score 7, target ~$872.00 (DD20d -11.6%)).

1 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 1 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

Data confidence FULL SIZE · Sharpe 2.39 [CI 1.48-3.33] · N=273 · WR 68% · PF 2.20 · p(Bonf) 0.000 · ½-Kelly 0.18 Full methodology →
🧠 Detects informed positioning by combining unusual options sweeps with next-day OI delta confirmation, EMA8/21 trend structure, and low-OI anomalies to isolate non-random flow.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $XRT $QQQ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1XRT ↑64 Watch$87.51 -0.62%BULLISH SWEEPCALL87.5$374KOPENING188167STRONG33
2QQQ ↑50 Watch$721.93 +1.31%BULLISH SWEEPCALL721$1589KNOT_FOUND—79STRONG47
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking shows only 5 resolved trades against 291 total signals, far below the 20-trade minimum for any meaningful read, so the reported 100% win rate is statistical noise, not confirmation of the backtest Sharpe of 2.39.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 292 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

One name today: NLY at a score of 59, Watch tier, flagged on a bullish sweep. The score distribution is as thin as it gets - a single signal sitting at exactly the median with nothing above 65, let alone 80. NLY is a mortgage REIT, and in a NEUTRAL macro regime (score 50.86) there is no structural tailwind to amplify the sweep signal. Watch tier plus a sub-65 score means conviction is low by this screener's own standards. If you trade it at all, size it small and wait for next-open OI delta to confirm before adding; the default read on this batch is weak, and patience beats forcing a single marginal name.

🧠 Ranks stocks by IV30 minus HV30 spread, adjusted for Sinclair sector bias and value factors, to identify the richest variance premium selling candidates.
Construction
IV30 vs HV30 spread + sector bias (Sinclair Table 4.7: Utilities +31%, Tech -8%) + value factors (P/E, RoE, mcap). Score 0-100 ranks premium-selling candidates.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape at 65% win rate and a +3.1% average versus SPY's -0.9% confirms the 2.34 backtest Sharpe is holding, and monthly cohorts moving from 50.8% in April to 60.3% in May to 80% in June show the edge accelerating, not decaying.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

NEE leads at 76, with KMB and XEL both at 75, and utilities filling five of the top eight slots, which is structurally expected given Sinclair's +31% utility premium adjustment. No name clears 80, the median sits at 66, and 39 of 44 signals cluster between 65 and 76, so the batch is tightly compressed rather than top-heavy. Patterns are null across the board, leaving score and sector alignment as the only differentiators. NEUTRAL macro at 50.86 is a flat backdrop, neither amplifying nor penalizing a defensive premium-selling book. Start with NEE at moderate conviction, it holds the highest score and the sector tailwind is structural, not situational.

🧠 Identifies tickers where implied volatility sits in the mid tertile of the variance risk premium, targeting options priced fairly relative to realized vol rather than at extremes of disbelief or catalyst saturation.
Construction
Longs tickers in the MID tertile of the variance risk premium (IV vs realized): options priced coherently with realized vol, neither too cheap (LOW = market disbelief) nor too rich (HIGH = catalyst already priced).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

CELH at 100 and TSCO at 99 lead the batch, both Very strong tier with no pattern data attached, so the signal is pure VRP positioning with no directional catalyst on record. CNX at 97 is the third name worth watching, a natural gas producer where mid-tertile VRP suggests the options market is not overpricing event risk. The distribution is top-heavy: 13 of 30 names score 80 or above and the median sits at 75, meaning today's batch has real concentration rather than a thin, scattered signal set. SDOW scoring 92 as a LONG is a flag, because SDOW is a 3x inverse Dow ETF and routing it through a long-biased screener requires a structural sanity check before trading. Macro is NEUTRAL at 50.86, which neither amplifies nor suppresses the setup. First preference is CELH at highest conviction, with TSCO as the cleaner second given it is an operating business rather than a leveraged inverse vehicle.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Every name in the batch scores 100 with no pattern data attached, so internal ranking is impossible and the screener is giving no differentiation today. DLR stands out as the most liquid, institutionally traded name and the most likely vehicle for a real hedging flow rather than thin-float noise. EGO adds a gold-miner angle that fits any residual macro-hedge thesis. The score distribution is maximally compressed: 49 of 50 signals score 80 or above, median sits at 99, which signals the filter is barely screening at all on this refresh. Neutral macro at 50.86 neither supports nor undermines the setup. If forced to act, DLR first at low conviction, and only after confirming the put flow is not just a dividend-arb artifact.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live record (57.5% WR, avg +1.7%, beating SPY -1.8% by 3.5 points) confirms the backtest direction but runs 7.5 points below the backtest's 65% win rate, and the monthly cohort trend recovering from a -3.3% February average to a +3.7% June average shows the screener tracking regime shifts rather than fighting them.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 58% · avg +1.7% · beats SPY -1.8% by +3.5 pts
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 492 resolved at +20d · WR 58% · avg +1.7% · vs SPY -1.8%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 497 closed · WR TB 35% · 13% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

CLFD, FEIM, and RAMP lead at 95, all Very strong tier with LAUNCH patterns, followed by ASYS, CVGI, DUOT, ENS, and NVCR at 90. The score distribution is uniformly compressed at the top: all 50 signals score 80 or above with a median of 85, leaving no weak tail to filter. The NEUTRAL macro regime (50.86) provides no directional tailwind, making this an alpha-specific setup rather than a macro-assisted trade. The sharpest number in today's data is the Very strong tier's 90-day live record: 0% win rate on 2 resolved trades at a -6.9% average, against 57-58% win rates for the Strong and Signal tiers. Prefer ASYS at 90 over any 95-score name at moderate conviction, sizing no larger than half-Kelly (10%) until the top tier accumulates a real live track record.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AD, LDOS, and NOG lead today at 86 each, all Strong tier, with BTU one point behind at 85 - no pattern field is populated for any name, so conviction rests on score and drawdown depth alone. The 28-signal batch clusters tightly from 65 to 86 with a median of 76 and nine names clearing 80, which is top-heavy rather than thin and suggests a reasonably concentrated opportunity set. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (50.86), which neither amplifies nor kills the contrarian thesis, though this screener is designed for a basket construct, not a single-name directional bet. Take AD and LDOS first as the highest-ranked names at moderate position size, LEAPS structure preferred per the backtest design, and treat each as a 1-2% allocation within a diversified basket rather than a standalone trade.

Data confidence FULL SIZE VVIX climax V3 STRICT_VALID, P(VIX spike) 11%->4% in 30d, wired prod (lib/vvix_climax_detector) Full methodology →
🧠 Detects vol-of-vol climax via VVIX percentile, VIX term structure, FOMC proximity, and weekend effect to flag sell-volatility entry points.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑62 Watch$749.01 +0.57%SELL VOL1.43
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape nominally confirms the screener (WR 62.5%, avg +1.48% vs SPY +1.43%), but the margin is essentially zero (+0.04 pts over SPY), and all the real edge lives in the seven Signal-tier trades at 85.7% WR and +2.73% avg, while the Watch tier's 17 trades average only +0.96% at a 53% win rate - today's Watch-only print falls squarely in the weaker half of the distribution.

So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Today's batch is thin: one name, SPY at score 61 in the Watch tier with no pattern tagged. Score 61 clears no meaningful threshold (n_ge_65 and n_ge_80 are both zero), so this is a marginal trigger, not a conviction setup. The single-name, single-score distribution is as concentrated as it gets, but concentrated at the low end. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 50.86 neither supports nor kills a vol-compression trade here, but it does nothing to upgrade a weak signal. Hold off, watch for a retrigger above 65 or a Signal-tier print before committing capital.

🧠 Flags stocks where options volume exceeds 5x the 20-day mean with premium above $50K and Vol/OI above 5, isolating calm, non-extended tickers where institutional sweep flow may signal directional intent.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

3FXI ↑52 Watch$32.42 +1.61%BULLISH SWEEPCALL3324$427K44.9026.5%0.40EARLY1560.22
4SPY ↑50 Watch$749.01 +0.57%BEARISH SWEEPPUT7426$421K3.8011.4%-0.22EARLY7320.16
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape posts 100% win rate and +189.2% average return against SPY at -0.6%, but only 23 of 620 total signals have resolved, making the sample too thin to override the backtest MARGINAL verdict - the monthly cohorts from March through June 2026 are uniformly 100% but carry the same small-sample caveat, so the key number to watch is n_resolved, not the win rate.

TQQQ scores 76 at Signal tier with a bullish sweep, the only pick that clears the session with real separation. SOXX and XLE both land at 65 on matching bullish sweeps, pairing semiconductors with energy in a reflationary read, though both sit at the Signal floor. The score distribution is soft - median 59, nothing above 80, three names at or above 65 - and seven of eight picks are ETFs, which dilutes individual-name conviction relative to a typical smart-flow batch. Macro is NEUTRAL at 50.86, providing no directional push. Lead with TQQQ as first look, moderate conviction only.

Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

XLY is the standout at 95 (Very Strong), the only name with real separation from the field. XLC and XLE both print 80 (Strong), forming a credible second tier, though pattern data is null across all picks, so no setup-level precision is available. Six of eleven sectors score 65 or above with three clearing 80, making the distribution moderately top-heavy with a wide mid-tier cluster, not thin but not decisive either. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 50.86, which is neither a headwind nor a tailwind for sector rotation. First preference is XLY, highest conviction of the batch, with XLC second at moderate sizing until pattern data populates.

🧠 Flags stocks down 40%+ from 52-week highs where capitulation volume and a reversal candle signal forced-seller exhaustion, targeting mean reversion within 15 days.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live 90-day win rate of 51.1% trails the 63.3% backtest by 12 points, but the +2.6% average beats SPY by 4.3 points, and the monthly cohort trend (January -12.9% avg to April +4.42% avg, June softening to +1.15%) confirms the screener is directionally valid even as the backtest itself carries a Bonferroni p-value of 0.60 and zero rigorous validation, meaning the live tier-split data, especially the Very strong tier's 19.5% average, is doing more work than the backtest stats ever could.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 51% · avg +2.5% · beats SPY -1.7% by +4.2 pts

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Detects sustained price trends by scoring annualized exponential-regression slope times R-squared, gated on SPY above its 200-day MA to filter regime.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

🧠 PEAD_5D flags post-earnings continuation setups 1-5 days after a surprise, scoring names on short-term momentum and relative volume for a tactical 5-day hold.
Construction
Tactical 5-day post-earnings continuation: scans for follow-through bars 1-5 days after surprise. Score from short-term momentum + relative volume.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Recent earnings with EPS surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Price ≥ $5, volume ≥ 500K, market cap ≥ $500M
  • Fixed 5-day hold
Score
min(100, surprise_pct × 4). LONG.

🧠 Detects intermarket correlation breaks (z >= 2 sigma, 7-day) across major pairs; two or more breaks fires a contrarian SPY long, severity scaling with break count.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 PEAD captures post-earnings price continuation in stocks that gap up on positive EPS surprises and hold the gap as analysts revise and late buyers pile in.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $BB
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1BB ↑95●●● Very strong$11.52 +0.04%50.0%420.7%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape, 61% win rate and avg +4.4% versus SPY at -0.1%, confirms the screener is earning its KEEP verdict and materially exceeds the MARGINAL backtest Sharpe of 0.5 to 0.6, though the June cohort's drop to 46% win rate is the one number that warrants watching as a potential regime shift.

Today's batch is a single name: BB at score 95, Very Strong tier, PRIMED pattern, LONG direction. 🧠 Detects large OTM options block trades over $200K with 1-10 DTE on quiet stocks, using next-day OI delta to confirm new accumulation versus position closing.
Construction
Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking flatly contradicts the old observational Sharpe: 41% WR at -5.4% average return versus SPY at -2.0% over 90 days, a -3.4 point lag that is the number ending the discussion, and the June cohort at -8.3% average deepens the deterioration seen since April's +8.4% peak, with the tier inversion - Very strong averaging -8.3% while Watch averages -1.8% - confirming that score rank carries no predictive gradient in real tape.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 488 closed · WR TB 22% · 11% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

JSML at 96 and CWAN at 93 are the standout names today, both Very strong tier and directionally LONG, though pattern fields are null so the thesis rests entirely on premium weight and OI confirmation rather than any identifiable setup. DXUV at 91 rounds out the top three Very strong prints. The 49-signal batch has a median score of 61 with only 7 names clearing 80, so the distribution is thin at the top but not artificially clustered. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 50.86 adds no tailwind. Given the KILL verdict on live tracking, conviction is low across the board; if forced to pick one, JSML at 96 is the first name but size it accordingly.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the KILL call: the Very strong tier posts a 53% win rate and +0.25% average return, which is the only tier not losing money, but the full-screener average of -1.1% across 489 resolved trades beats SPY by just 0.7 points in a down tape, and May and June monthly cohorts both repeat that same -1.13% average, showing no improvement in the trend.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 50 signals score 100 with a median of 100 and every name clearing the 80 threshold, which is a score ceiling artifact, not signal quality. BA and ALGN are the names most likely to draw attention given sector breadth, and ANF adds consumer discretionary exposure, but none carry a pattern tag so the thesis rests solely on the cross itself. The distribution is not informative here since it is pinned at the maximum for every ticker. The NEUTRAL macro regime (50.86) neither supports nor opposes momentum names, but it offers no tailwind either. Given the screener carries a KILL verdict in live tracking, the correct preference is to hold off entirely rather than force conviction from a batch with no differentiation.

🧠 Identifies stocks where heavy short positioning meets forced covering pressure: high float short interest above 20%, cost-to-borrow spikes, and a price breakout above a key moving average converge to create a mechanical squeeze setup.
Construction
High short interest (>20% float) + cost-to-borrow spike + technical breakout above key MA. Score blends SI %, days-to-cover, CTB, and price momentum.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape at 56% win rate and +3.9% avg versus SPY at -2.0% confirms the screener generates real alpha against a weak benchmark, and the June cohort extending that edge at +5.4% avg reinforces the KEEP verdict, but the tier inversion - Very Strong losing 4.95% on average versus Watch winning 5.05% - is the number that matters most and directly contradicts any assumption that higher scores mean better outcomes here.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ACHC, BEAM, BV, and DFH all print 95 with PRIMED pattern, the highest scores in today's batch of 50, but the tracking data carries a critical warning: the Very Strong tier is the worst-performing bucket in live results, with a 40.9% win rate and avg return of -4.95%, meaning the top-scored names are historically the most dangerous to chase. The distribution is top-heavy, with 18 of 50 signals at 80 or above and every signal clearing 65, so there is no thin tail today, just a crowded upper end. Macro is neutral at 50.86, which neither validates nor kills squeeze setups but removes a regime tailwind. Given the tier inversion, ABEO or ABX at 85 in the Strong bucket - where live avg is +2.2% - are more actionable than the four 95-scorers; take ABEO first at low conviction until the Very Strong tier stops bleeding.

🧠 Stocks ranked by relative volume across three lookback windows multiplied by multi-horizon price momentum, filtering for a positive 20-day trend.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Detects mean-reversion setups where rejection tails of 1.8x ATR or more form at Market Profile value-area extremes, signaling failed auction and likely return into value.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $GLRE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1GLRE ↑100●●● Very strong$16.48 +0.64%LOW2.0928.5%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The tape is too thin to confirm or contradict anything: 3 resolved trades carry a 66.67% win rate and a 3.85% avg 20-day return against a SPY baseline of 0.056%, which directionally matches the filtered backtest edge, but the WATCH verdict stands because the key number is N=3 against a required minimum of 20.

Data confidence STARTER PEAD gap-up + weekly trigger, small N, mechanically sound but too early for a verdict Full methodology →
🧠 Catches post-earnings continuation by buying the first weekly red-candle pullback that closes above the prior week's high within four weeks of the gap.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.

#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayGapVol ratioBars ago

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Opening gap on one of the last 3 bars: 5.0% ≤ gap < 10.0%

Score
gap_pct × 15 (not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled, top pick = 100). LONG.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Three signals today, all long: AMPL scores a perfect 100 in the Very strong tier, making it the lead candidate, though a null pattern field strips any setup context beyond the score itself. APPX prints 87 in the Strong tier and is a workable second. ALSN at 78 sits in the Signal tier, which is the best-performing bucket in 90-day live tracking at 100% WR and avg +15.3%, but that result rests on only five trades, so weight it accordingly. The score distribution is top-heavy, with all three signals above 65 and a median of 87, but three total names is a thin batch. Macro registers NEUTRAL at 50.86, offering no directional tailwind. AMPL first on score at medium conviction, thin batch and a neutral tape limit the press.

🧠 Filters SMART_FLOW entries by skipping signals where KVO or Force Index shows a volume-momentum conflict within a 3-day window, leaving only cleaner flow reads.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

One name today: TLT at a score of 60, Watch tier, BULLISH SWEEP pattern, LONG direction. That is the entire batch - n_signals equals 1, median equals max equals 60, nothing clears 65. The distribution is not clustered or thin in any interesting way; it is effectively empty. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 50.86 neither supports nor undermines a long duration trade, and TLT at Watch-tier with a mid-range score is not a high-conviction setup. If forced to act, TLT is the only option, but the bar should be a confirmed intraday sweep with size before sizing in, and conviction is low.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape flatly contradicts the backtest: 90-day resolved trades show a 43% win rate and a mean return of -2.8% versus SPY at -0.9%, a -1.9 point gap, and the June 2026 cohort makes it worse with a 27% win rate and -14.7% average, reversing the April high-water mark of 54.9% and +12.7% - the deterioration trend and the KILL verdict confirm this screener has no live edge.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The batch is dominated by semiconductor and storage hardware names: STX and WDC (storage) and LRCX and AMAT (equipment) all score 100 with a Very Strong tier, but every single one of 50 signals sits at or above 80, with a median of 99 and a max of 100. The score distribution is completely ceiling-pinned, which means the model cannot differentiate within today's batch at all. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 50.86 neither supports nor undermines a momentum tilt, but the screener's backtest is formally REJECTED - the headline return was a leveraged-ETF artifact and true alpha is -0.8%/yr at p=0.25. Given that, treat any name here as a watchlist candidate only, not a sized position; if forced to pick first, LRCX fits a semis-equipment rotation thesis with the most institutional visibility, at low conviction.

🧠 Flags stocks with weekly RSI(14) below 30 then confirms via daily RSI crossover, candlestick reversal, and demethod zone scoring to capture near-term mean-reversion bounces.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.
Top picks: $DKL $BORR $KR

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape returns a KEEP verdict with a 56.7% win rate and +1.6% avg outpacing SPY by 0.3 points, but the monthly cohort trend directly contradicts that headline: June 2026 has collapsed to a 34.8% win rate and -6.15% avg return, reversing sharply from March's 78.7% win rate and April's +5.3% avg, which means the live edge is deteriorating fast and the aggregate 90-day number is being carried by older, now-stale cohorts.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

DLNG tops the batch at 70 (Signal tier, SETUP pattern) and is the only name worth putting first given the compressed scoring landscape. DDL at 68 and DKL at 66 round out the Signal tier and are the only other names with any structural conviction. With zero signals above 80, a median of 59, and just 5 names clearing 65 out of 31 total, the distribution is thin and top-heavy at a mediocre ceiling. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (50.86), offering no directional tailwind to pad a weak reversal setup. Preference is DLNG first at reduced size, Signal tier only, with DDL and DKL as secondary watches if DLNG triggers cleanly.

🧠 Flags unusual volume, OI delta, and premium divergence on crypto ETFs to front-run near-term directional moves in spot crypto proxies via institutional flow.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.
Top picks: $HUT $BITO $FBTC
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSIRS IBIT 20dRegime
1HUT ↑75● Signal$107.87 +11.05%41.23-12.3%neutral
2BITO ↑50 Watch$8.35 +0.07%44.00-0.5%neutral
3FBTC ↑50 Watch$53.55 +0.05%44.68-0.2%neutral
4IBIT ↑50 Watch$34.84 -0.09%44.550.0%neutral
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.

MARA is the only name worth attention, scoring 80 as the sole Strong-tier TRIGGER in today's six-name batch. HUT at 60 is next but sits squarely inside Watch territory alongside BITO, FBTC, and IBIT, all clustered at 55. Score distribution is thin and top-heavy: five of six names compress into a 50-60 range and only one name clears 65. Macro is NEUTRAL at 50.86, providing no regime tailwind for crypto beta longs. Strong-tier signals in live tracking carry a 0% win rate and -13.2% average return, making MARA's TRIGGER historically the most dangerous entry in this screener - pass on the full batch.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SMIN leads at 74 and WCLD follows at 73, both tagged Signal/LONG with no pattern data attached, so the thesis rests entirely on score rank rather than any chart setup. PPA at 71 adds a defense-adjacent angle. The distribution is compressed and uninspiring: max of 74, median of 61, zero names clearing 80, and only 15 of 50 above 65. That top-heaviness is shallow, not a sign of conviction. Macro sits at NEUTRAL with a score of 50.86, which neither validates nor kills a theme-momentum bet, but removes any regime tailwind. Take SMIN first given the top score, but treat this as low-conviction sizing only.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 8 top picks today score 100 and sit at the Very strong tier - BL, CELH, HDV, MAZE, QDEL, RNA, SRTY, and TNDM - but pattern data is null across the board, leaving the thesis thin on specifics. SRTY is a 3x inverse small-cap ETF, which is an unusual inclusion in a long-biased batch and warrants scrutiny before treating it as a conventional equity setup. The score distribution is top-heavy: 12 of 50 signals reach 80 or above, median sits at 70, and 41 of 50 clear the 65 threshold, so the batch has real density at the high end. Macro is NEUTRAL at 50.86, which neither tailwinds nor headwinds the list. Given that the Very strong tier historically prints only 3.4% avg vs 9.5% for Watch, and no patterns are available to differentiate, conviction is low - if forced to pick one, CELH has sector momentum context, but treat this batch as Watch-grade until pattern data populates.

AVGO at 79 is the strongest Signal-tier name but sits well below the top two. NEUTRAL macro at 50.86 neither supports nor penalizes the long bias here, so this is a stock-picker's batch, not a regime trade. Start with ASML first at high conviction; treat FB as secondary but flag it - FB is Meta's retired ticker and points to a data hygiene issue worth resolving before sizing.

SVRE, JEF, and LIME all score 100 in the Very Strong tier and are the lead names today, though pattern data is null across every pick so conviction rests entirely on insider dollar size and C-suite rank. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 50.86 is a muted backdrop that neither confirms nor undermines the insider-conviction thesis. First call is JEF at 100, medium conviction, on superior liquidity and institutional familiarity relative to the small-cap names clustered around it.

🧠 Flags individual stocks lagging their sector move, scoring by sector relative strength multiplied by stock underperformance, to capture delayed catch-up momentum.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

Today's batch is weak and undifferentiated. All 16 signals score exactly 50, which is simultaneously the max and the median, meaning the scoring function produced no spread whatsoever across the cohort. Among the more recognizable names, ZTS is a large-cap compounder in animal health and TLRY carries cannabis sector exposure, but neither has a score, tier, or pattern that distinguishes it from the other six names listed. Pass on the entire batch until a clear leader separates from the pack.

Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Ranks US equities by EV/EBIT ascending to surface the cheapest operating-earnings names, betting on mean reversion as depressed multiples reprice toward fair value.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With zero resolved trades in 90 days and no monthly cohorts, there is no live tape to confirm or contradict anything; the only number on the board is a 60% win rate across 15 tiebreak trades, which is too thin and too early to treat as signal.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEinsufficient data (0 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 53 signals captured
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 18 closed · WR TB 56% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BZ leads the batch at score 100 and WRD follows at 97, both Very Strong tier, making them the first two names to examine for the classic Acquirer's Multiple setup of compressed EV-to-EBIT. GPC at 90 is the third notable name, a large-cap industrials compounder that rarely screens this cheap on operating earnings. The 16-signal batch is top-heavy: 7 of 16 names score above 80 and the median sits at 73, so the day's output is concentrated at the genuinely cheap end rather than broadly dispersed junk. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 50.86 is not a headwind but provides no particular tailwind for contrarian deep value, which historically needs either a catalyst or a patient holding window to work. BZ is the first look, at low conviction only, given the screener carries no backtest and zero resolved tracking trades to anchor sizing.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (WR 60%, avg +0.04%, beating SPY by 0.6 points on a -0.55% SPY baseline) supports the KEEP verdict, but the April 2026 cohort (WR 16.7%, avg -15.9%) is a live warning that the screener can blow up badly in the wrong regime, and the backtest remains formally unvalidated at N=17 versus the required N=30.

CART and CF both print perfect 100s at Very Strong tier, making them the lead candidates; BKNG at 99 is a close third and adds a higher-profile name to the set. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (50.86), which neither supports nor undercuts a short bias, so this is not a high-conviction tape for a hedge screener. Take CART first given the perfect score and Very Strong tier, but size conservatively until the regime shifts bearish.

🧠 Detects stocks where 3 or more insiders buy within 30 days, weighting cluster size, total dollar value, and C-suite rank to surface informed accumulation.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.

Every signal in today's batch prints at exactly 60 - the score distribution is fully compressed with zero names at 65 or above and a median equal to the max, which makes differentiation impossible on score alone. ADSK and FISV stand out as the highest-quality franchises in the set given their size and institutional coverage, meaning insider conviction there is harder to fake than in smaller names like FCBM or FNWD where thin float can distort the signal. KMX is the most macro-sensitive name here and sits in a consumer cycle that is softening, making it lower conviction. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 50.86 offers no tailwind. This is a weak batch: no pattern data populated, no tier above Watch, and conviction is thin. If forced to act, ADSK first, half-sized.

🧠 Flags macro ETFs passing both Antonacci tests: positive 12-month absolute return and top relative rank versus peers, capturing trend-leadership rotation across asset classes.
Construction
Antonacci dual momentum on a macro ETF set: absolute (12m return > 0) AND relative (top rank vs peers). Long the leaders.

🧠 Ranks tickers by 20-day RS vs SPY, sector breadth, and cluster score to surface top relative-strength names inside bullish sectors.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.

#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1AAT ↑82●● Strong$25.46 -0.18%Real Estate19.0%31.5%

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No backtest stats exist yet (N=0, needs 30 resolved trades), leaving the 90-day live tape as the only validation reference: WR 55.4% and avg +4.3% versus SPY at -1.76% for a +6.1-point edge, a figure echoed by the single May 2026 cohort at 55.7% WR and +10.8% average, enough to support the KEEP verdict on live data alone.

EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects bear and bull traps by flagging a close outside the value area that is immediately reclaimed the next day, trapping countertrend participants.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $AMX $AVXL $ALV
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1AMX ↑65● Signal$25.90 +0.70%BEAR_TRAP25.6226.420.66
2AVXL ↑65● Signal$2.74 -0.72%BEAR_TRAP2.532.690.17
3ALV ↑60 Watch$117.48 +1.07%BEAR_TRAP114.78122.133.27
4AMLP ↑55 Watch$52.15 +0.17%BEAR_TRAP51.4052.740.78
5ASM ↑55 Watch$6.72 +2.28%BEAR_TRAP6.397.120.45
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (WR 71%, avg +4.7% vs SPY -1.1%) confirms the KEEP verdict and suggests the mechanism has real signal, but June's cohort (WR 50%, avg -5.2%) is the contradicting data point that matters most right now - the Watch tier's outsized 90-day average of +7.4% drove the headline number, and the Signal tier resolved at only 50% WR and +0.4% avg, which is the tier today's batch sits in.

🧠 Detects fake breakouts above the Value Area High that close back inside, trapping longs and generating a short entry on the failure.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.

Exact criteria & score formula

Score

sizing guide, not permission

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Catches pre-earnings straddle IV underpricing by comparing implied vol to realized expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20, 3 to 14 days before the event.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $JPM $ASML
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1JPM ↑65● Signal$337.90 +1.03%PRE_IV_RAMP11—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
2ASML ↑55 Watch$1829.05 +3.38%PRE_IV_RAMP12—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

JPM is the lead name at 75, the only ticker with any score separation from the rest of the batch. GE and WFC both sit at 65 with no pattern context available, as that field is null across all three names. The distribution is narrow and low-conviction - three signals packed between 65 and 75, zero clearing 80 - which makes today a thin batch by any honest read. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 50.86 provides no directional tailwind to amplify vol expansion trades, so any edge here is purely mechanical and event-specific. JPM first, small size, at low-to-moderate conviction given its score lead and the absence of corroborating pattern data.

EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects unusual put-skew spikes versus a 90-day baseline as a contrarian long signal when the underlying price action is calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $COST $MSTR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1COST ↓91●●● Very strong$952.28 +0.06%77%75—SELL CALL/PUT (IV rich)
2MSTR ↑55 Watch$96.18 -4.55%66%1000.03BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With the formal backtest not yet valid at N=10, the 90-day tape (54% WR, +9.0% avg, +7.7pts vs SPY) and the May cohort (n=57, 57.9% WR, +10.1% avg) together confirm the aggregate edge, but the critical number is the tier inversion: Watch names run +15.1% avg at 66.7% WR while Very Strong names sit at -5.1% avg and 16.7% WR, which structurally downgrades today's higher-scoring MU and MSTR relative to the lower-tier Watch trio.

🧠 Flags stocks where put IV exceeds call IV at above-90th-percentile skew, detecting concentrated demand for downside protection as a directional signal.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$382.08 +6.00%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Nine resolved trades produce a 55.6% overall win rate but a -5.5% average 20-day return against SPY's +0.83%, the damage concentrated entirely in the Strong tier (0% win rate, -55.9% average across 2 trades), while the April 2026 cohort's -88.8% worst trade confirms the tail risk here is real and the WATCH verdict is the right call until N reaches at least 20.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags tickers where 30-day implied vol exceeds 20-day realized vol by more than 1.5 standard deviations, surfacing inflated fear premiums suitable for options premium selling.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $COST
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1COST ↓100●●● Very strong$952.28 +0.06%77%23%3.35EXTREME_FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (315 resolved, WR 44%, avg +1.8% vs SPY +1.1%) is barely ahead of the index, and the monthly cohort trend is the key concern - April printed a 55.9% WR and +10.8% avg, May collapsed to -0.22%, and the small June sample sits at -1.5%, suggesting recent signal quality has deteriorated sharply and the backtest edge, which is formally unverified at N=25, is not confirmed by live tracking.

🧠 Composite options screener combining IV rank, IV30/HV20 ratio, skew, term structure, and institutional flow signals to flag volatility mispricing in US equities.
Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.
Top picks: $VXX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1VXX ↓85●● Strong$21.64 -1.79%54%861%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

Altman

ALTMANEOD⚠ Dormant · 2026-05-23 (27d)
Live: base version (no variants documented)

Data confidence
STARTER · untested

EOD
Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 ALTMAN screens US equities using Altman Z-score derived financial distress signals to surface turnaround or short candidates at scoring extremes.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is empty. top_picks_today returns no candidates, and the score distribution shows one signal at a score of zero, with nothing clearing 65 or 80. That is not a batch worth trading. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (50.86), which offers no directional tailwind to compensate for the absence of quality signals. No actionable preference today - the screener is dry.

🧠 STFR screens for short-sale candidates using proprietary criteria defined in the source script; edge mechanism undisclosed from available data.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 VSA (Volume Spread Analysis) flags institutional accumulation or distribution by cross-referencing price spread, close location, and volume against prior bars.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
